question_text: Explain the difference between average speed and instantaneous speed.
correct_answer: Average speed is the total distance divided by total time for a whole journey, while instantaneous speed is the speed at one exact moment in time.
explanation: When you travel, your speed changes constantly – instantaneous speed is what a speedometer reads at any moment, but average speed smooths out all those changes over the whole trip. For example, a car's average speed on a motorway journey might be 60 m/s, but at one instant it might be going 70 m/s.
